title: Return a better error message when failed to resolve an image
body:
During the creation of a new builder object there are errors that are only logged into `logrus.Debugf`.

If in the end of the process `ref` or `img` are `nil` and options.FromImage` is set then it means that there was an issue. By default, it was assumed that the image name is wrong.
Yet, this assumption isn't always correct. For example, it might fail due to authorization or connection errors.

In this patch, I am attempting to fix this problem by checking the last error stored in the `err` variable and returning the cause of the failure.

Fixes #401

comments.body:
@TomSweeneyRedHat thank you for noticing this!
I will look at this.
OK, I updated the code. PTAL.

The failure in Travis is not related to the commit:
> The command "sudo apt-get update" failed and exited with 100 during .
Tests now pass.  @TomSweeneyRedHat can you confirm that you like the current handling.

The end result looks very good on the tests now:
```
########
# Use bad password on from/pull, this should fail
########
buildah from localhost:5000/my-commit-alpine --pull-always --cert-dir /root/auth  --tls-verify=true --creds=testuser:badpassword
unauthorized: authentication required
```
I'm a little concerned with the hack to make it so, but not sure there's much choice.  @nalind WDYT?

If @nalind is hip, I'm hip.
@TomSweeneyRedHat I will try to open a PR to `containers/images` to return an `errcode` in that part so we could remove the hack.

Ok this is better then what we have.  Merging
@rh-atomic-bot r+
